---
title: "Ingest data"
description: "From raw sequencing output to a per-sample count matrix (H5MU)."
categories: [openpipeline]
order: 10
---

[`openpipeline`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline}<span class="swatch openpipeline"></span>

Ingestion turns the raw output of a sequencing run into a per-sample **count
matrix** stored as an `.h5mu` file: reads are aligned to a reference, assigned to
cells by barcode, and counted once per molecule by UMI. That object is the input
to [sample processing](process-samples.qmd).

## Overview

Ingestion is at most three steps; run only the ones you need:

1. **Demultiplex, only if you start from raw Illumina BCL.** `ingestion/demux`
   turns BCL into per-sample FASTQs. Skip it if your provider already delivered
   FASTQs.
2. **Build a reference, if you don't have one.** `ingestion/make_reference`
   builds the genome index the mapper needs. Skip it if you already have a
   pre-built reference.
3. **Map.** Pick the mapper for your platform:
   - **10x Genomics:** `ingestion/cellranger_mapping` (gene expression only) or
     `ingestion/cellranger_multi` (multiple libraries: GEX + VDJ + ADT/HTO).
   - **BD Rhapsody:** `ingestion/bd_rhapsody`.

| Workflow | Use when |
|---|---|
| [`ingestion/demux`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/workflows/ingestion/demux.qmd) | Starting from raw Illumina BCL files (run first) |
| [`ingestion/make_reference`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/workflows/ingestion/make_reference.qmd) | Building the reference genome index the mapper needs |
| [`ingestion/cellranger_mapping`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/workflows/ingestion/cellranger_mapping.qmd) | 10x, gene expression only |
| [`ingestion/cellranger_multi`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/workflows/ingestion/cellranger_multi.qmd) | 10x, multiple libraries (GEX + VDJ + ADT/HTO) |
| [`ingestion/bd_rhapsody`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/workflows/ingestion/bd_rhapsody.qmd) | BD Rhapsody data |

Not sure which fits? The [Available workflows](../workflows/index.qmd) page shows
these in pipeline context.

## Example: 10x multi-library mapping

For example, to map a 10x sample with gene expression, VDJ, and antibody-capture
libraries jointly, `ingestion/cellranger_multi` takes a `param_list` where each
entry's `input` / `library_id` / `library_type` lists line up row-for-row:

```yaml
# params.yaml
param_list:
  - id: sample_A
    input:
      - fastq/sample_A_gex/*_R*.fastq.gz
      - fastq/sample_A_vdj/*_R*.fastq.gz
      - fastq/sample_A_adt/*_R*.fastq.gz
    library_id: [sample_A_gex, sample_A_vdj, sample_A_adt]
    library_type: [Gene Expression, VDJ-T, Antibody Capture]
gex_reference: reference_gex/
vdj_reference: reference_vdj/
feature_reference: feature_reference.csv
publish_dir: results/ingestion
```

```bash
nextflow run https://packages.viash-hub.com/vsh/openpipeline.git \
  -r 4.2.0 -latest -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/workflows/ingestion/cellranger_multi/main.nf \
  -params-file params.yaml
```

## Already have a count matrix?

If your data is already quantified but not in `.h5mu`, convert it, no mapping
needed:

| Format you have | Convert with |
|---|---|
| Cell Ranger `multi` output | [`convert/from_cellranger_multi_to_h5mu`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/convert/from_cellranger_multi_to_h5mu.qmd) |
| 10x MEX (`.mtx`) | [`convert/from_10xmtx_to_h5mu`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/convert/from_10xmtx_to_h5mu.qmd) |
| AnnData (`.h5ad`) | [`convert/from_h5ad_to_h5mu`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/convert/from_h5ad_to_h5mu.qmd) |
| Seurat | [`convert/from_seurat_to_h5mu`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/convert/from_seurat_to_h5mu.qmd) |
| TileDB | [`convert/from_tiledb_to_h5mu`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/convert/from_tiledb_to_h5mu.qmd) |

::: {.callout-tip}
For Cell Ranger data, convert from the **full output directory**
(`from_cellranger_multi_to_h5mu`), not a bare `.h5` matrix. The full output
carries the cell and feature metadata (feature types, per-sample metrics) that a
standalone `.h5` drops, so downstream steps annotate correctly.
:::

## Key choices

- **Which mapper** depends on platform and library type, see the overview table.
- **Reference genome:** build one with `ingestion/make_reference`, or point at a
  pre-built reference directly.
- **A whole cohort in one run:** pass a [`param_list`](process-many-samples.qmd)
  with one entry per sample.

## Output

Each sample becomes one `.h5mu` file holding one or more modalities, depending on
the libraries:

| Modality key | Content |
|---|---|
| `rna` | RNA count matrix (cells × genes) |
| `prot` | ADT count matrix, antibody-derived tags (CITE-seq) |
| `vdj_b` / `vdj_t` | Immune repertoire (B/T cell receptor sequences) |
| `atac` | Chromatin accessibility (ATAC-seq peaks) |
| `gdo` | Guide RNA / CRISPR perturbation data |

Raw counts sit in `.X`, cell metadata in `.obs`, feature metadata in `.var`. No
filtering has been applied yet, the matrix holds both real cells and empty
droplets. Next: [sample processing](process-samples.qmd).

:::{.callout-tip}
The multimodal object and its slots (`.X`, `.obs`, `.var`, `.obsm`, `.mod`) are
explained in [MuData](../concepts/mudata.qmd).
:::
